data.body:
SESSION SUMMARY (2026-07-15) — E1 e95e-1543-cac7-47b9 (doctrinal-untruthful-vaquita), in_progress under RebarBotNava, worktree corpus-e1. PAUSED at the human-adjudication step. Full resume prompt written to docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/HANDOFF.md.

OPERATOR-APPROVED SCOPE EXPANSION: replaced the LLM-only Cohen's-kappa>=0.7 ship/discard gate with (1) honest stratified kappa reporting (surfaced/dropped + Gwet's AC1 + confusion matrix; NOT a hard gate) and (2) HUMAN ADJUDICATION of the contested findings as the authoritative validity basis. Also asked to GROUP similar disputes to avoid repetitive adjudication.

DONE THIS SESSION:
- Discovered 15% of sampled adjudication findings were TEXT-LESS (pre-story-4e19/e344 lean sidecar schema; the finding-prose is only persisted since then; unrecoverable). Added a text-bearing inclusion filter to build_adjudication_corpus.collect(); REBUILT the corpus as a clean seed-1729 sample (300 surfaced + 100 dropped, 0 text-less). Text-bearing population is ample (15,821 surfaced / 10,850 dropped).
- Re-labeled: rater_a 399/400 (opus-4-8), rater_b 102/110 subset (sonnet-5).
- Fixed 3 harness bugs: adjudicate.py single-terminal-write -> incremental atomic checkpointing (CHECKPOINT_EVERY=5); adjudicate.py max_tokens 400->2000 (models think by default -> 400 fully consumed by thinking -> empty output; THIS, not rate limiting, caused the persistent label failures); cluster_disputes._classify (blank rater_b was mis-flagged as a dispute; a dispute needs BOTH raters to have judged).
- kappa.py now stratifies by source + reports Gwet's AC1 + confusion (reporting, not gate; --strict restores old behavior). New harnesses: cluster_disputes.py (dispute clustering + worksheet), apply_adjudications.py (fold human verdicts -> final_label).

HONEST RELIABILITY (clean corpus, 94 usable pairs): raw agreement 0.766, Cohen's kappa 0.47, Gwet's AC1 0.58. surfaced 0.80/0.56/0.62; dropped 0.70/0.23/0.52. Low kappa = the kappa paradox (raters lean ~65-69% TP; the gate mostly surfaces real defects + drops genuine noise). AC1 shows moderate agreement. This is WHY human adjudication (not a kappa gate) is the validity basis.

DISPUTES: 30 total (22 hard TP<->FP + 8 soft one-rater-abstained) in runs/disputes.jsonl + runs/dispute_worksheet.md.

GROUPING FINDING (operator's key question): the disputes are almost entirely IRREDUCIBLE. cluster_disputes.py's text-Jaccard>=0.82 within (source,criterion) collapsed 0. An empirical same-source stopword-filtered Jaccard>=0.20 scan found only ONE genuinely groupable pair: H013(G3) ~ H015(G7) @0.40 (both 'parent epic imposes a mandatory advisory-disposition gate on child stories'). So token-based grouping saves ~1 decision; a deeper LLM-semantic grouping could catch differently-worded thematic repeats (e.g. H003/H004 'no proving command in ACs') if the operator wants it. Was interrupted while presenting the disputes as AskUserQuestion (4/batch) to clarify grouping first.

NEXT (see HANDOFF.md): decide grouping -> human-adjudicate the 30 disputes (genuine input, never fabricate) -> apply_adjudications.py fold -> finalize README (KAPPA-RESULT placeholder) + update E1 ACs (kappa reported + [operator-attested] human-adjudication; drop the kappa>=0.7 gate; note text-bearing inclusion) -> pre-flight make lint/typecheck/test (docs-only) -> commit ONLY the specific E1 files (NEVER git add runs/) as bot+DCO+rebar-ticket trailer -> Gerrit (install commit-msg hook) -> LLM-Review+1 AND Verified+1 -> Submit -> close E1 from the worktree via completion verifier.
